channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: GM is pulling the funding for its Cruise robotaxi company
item.description: General Motors is pulling funding from robotaxi company Cruise in order to focus on its own autonomous and assisted-driving systems.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Go eclipses Node.js in web API requests, Cloudflare reports
item.description: Go has surpassed Node.js as the most popular language used for making automated API requests, according to cloud provider Cloudflare’s annual review of internet trends.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why olive oil girl is TikTok’s main character
item.description: If you’ve spent any time on TikTok in the past few days, you’ve probably come across the viral olive oil story. If you haven’t, strap in. The latest trend on the platform sees people sharing their wildest stories over a slideshow of images of Pepe the King Prawn soundtracked to Like A Prayer.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 is the year of quantum science; what that’s all about
item.description: In celebration of the 100th anniversary of the discovery of quantum mechanics, United Nations has declared 2025 as the International Year of Quantum Science and Technology.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 5G never delivered for cloud computing
item.description: I recall this as if it was yesterday. I entered the conference room to talk about cloud computing and 5G. I gave this type of presentation often a few years ago. The executives were excited about 5G’s potential. I took a deep breath, knowing I had to voice my concerns.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia have slipped after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ott, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Philippine volcano eruption sends villagers fleeing for safety as homes are blanketed in ash
item.description: A large volcano eruption in the Philippines sent a plume of ash and gases up to 1.8 miles high following a powerful blast that sent villagers fleeing to emergency shelters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Low-tech solutions to high-tech cybercrimes
item.description: You might hear that 2025 will be the Year of artificial intelligence (AI) cybercrime. But the trend really began in 2024. AI crime will prove so overwhelming that some say the only way to fight it is through AI security software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder: How Silicon Valley protects its CEOs
item.description: Trying to keep tech leaders safe in a country awash with guns, and in an industry that can generate volatile emotions, does not come cheaply.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: IAEA chief: Iran is poised to 'quite dramatically' increase stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium
item.description: MANAMA, Bahrain (AP) — Iran is poised to “quite dramatically” increase its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium as it has started cascades of advanced centrifuges, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency warned Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO uncorks anger at insurance industry
item.description: The killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on a Manhattan street is the latest moment to shine a light on the tensions roiling American life.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 is now in an initial rampdown phase, with the feature set frozen at 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency. JDK 24 reached the rampdown phase on December 5. It is due as a production release on March 18, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cisco strengthens AWS integration to speed troubleshooting in multicloud environments
item.description: Cisco this week said it has bolstered integrations between its own hybrid cloud offerings and Amazon Web Services (AWS) to improve visibility and help enterprise customers more accurately troubleshoot application availability issues across multicloud networks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White House says at least 8 US telecom firms, dozens of nations impacted by China hacking campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top White House official on Wednesday said at least eight U.S. telecom firms and dozens of nations have been impacted by a Chinese hacking campaign.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: District of Columbia says Amazon secretly stopped fast deliveries to 2 predominantly Black ZIP codes
item.description: The District of Columbia sued Amazon on Wednesday, alleging the company secretly stopped providing its fastest delivery service to residents of two predominantly Black neighborhoods while still charging millions of dollars for a membership that promises the benefit.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kyndryl, Nokia expand AI, quantum-safe connectivity options
item.description: Kyndryl is extending its relationship with Nokia and expanding its menu of integration services by including more of Nokia’s data center networking products.
item.dc:creator: Network World
